rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANTs TOM COLLINS, HARRY GILBERT & ST. PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is off calendar as moot. Defendant filed the motion on November 15, 2012, and that motion seeks to dismiss the Second Amended Complaint. On February 4, 2013, Plaintiff filed a Third Amended Complaint that moots the instant motion. In State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131, the court provides: Because there is but one complaint in a civil action [citation], the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint. [citation] Thus, once an amended complaint is filed, it is error to grant summary adjudication on a cause of action contained in a previous complaint. [citation] As our colleagues in Division Two have explained, "a court granting plaintiff leave to amend a cause of action should not at the same time attempt to summarily adjudicate material issues which underlie that same cause of action. After a cause of action is amended, the court may rule in favor of the defendant if, upon subsequent motion, or perhaps renewal of the earlier motion if appropriately framed, it is shown ... there are no triable material issues of fact which would permit recovery on that theory." (citations and italics omitted) This ruling does not prejudice defendant's right to challenge the Third Amended Complaint by subsequent motion or demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Post Construction Services, L.P., A California, South San Francisco I, L.L.C., A California, George Cresson, Greater Sonoma Partnership, Loanvest I, Llc, A California Limited Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 4, Defendants Post Construction Services, L.P., A California, South San Francisco I, L.L.C., A California, George Cresson, Greater Sonoma Partnership & Loanvest I, Llc's Motion For Summary Adjudication. The motion will be heard by Judge Peter Busch in Department 504 at 2:30 p.m. on Friday, March 22, 2013. The motion for summary adjudication as to rescission is denied. It is an improper motion to reconsider in that it requests exactly the same relief on the basis of exactly the same document as the prior motion. Although premised on a different signature line in that document, the argument was available to and could and should have been raised by defendants in their prior motion. In any event, there remains a triable issue of fact as to the effectiveness of the attorney's signature in light of the arguable ambiguity as to the party on whose behalf the letter was sent given the party signature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant George Cresson, South Bay Real Estate Commerce Group, Llc, Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 4, Defendants George Cresson & South Bay Real Estate Commerce Group, Llc's Motion For Summary Judgment. The motion will be heard by Judge Peter Busch in Department 504 at 2:30 p.m. on Friday, March 22, 2013. The motion for summary adjudication is granted with respect to the claim for breach of fiduciary duty because the gravamen of the claim is injury to the entity, and plaintiff lacks standing to sue on the entity's behalf for injuries to the entity. Injuries, if any, to the plaintiff are indirect. The motion is denied as to the request for declaratory relief. There remain issues for trial at least because the trial court, considering all the circumstances, could agree to allow amendment to address the effect of the more recent actions, and there remain issues as to prior actions notwithstanding plaintiff's prior statements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LONG VS. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant San Francisco Forty Niners, Limited'S Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting The Depositions Of Dr. John York, Jed York And Trent Baalke
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 5, Defendant San Francisco Forty Niners, Limited'S Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting The Depositions Of Dr. John York, Jed York And Trent Baalke Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction And Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT COLLEEN HOLLAND's Motion For Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction And Appointment Of Receiver. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to grant the preliminary injunction and appoint a receiver. The parties are to meet and confer regarding nominating a receiver. The receiver will have broad powers but as a practical matter exercise limited authority. The Court denies Mr. Connelly's request for a mutual injunction without prejudice. The record fails to show that Ms. Holland has abused her authority. If she does so, Mr. Connelly may apply for injunctive relief. The Court agrees with Mr. Connelly that the parties should coordinate their business activities. The Court denies Mr. Connelly's other suggestions, e.g., parties must show mutual respect, parties must not make public comments, as vague, overbroad and/or an unconstitutional prior restraint of speech. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD BAKER VS. ADULT ENTERTAINMENT BROADCAST NETWORK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF RONALD BAKER's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. The motion to file the second amended complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and allowing plaintiff to file the pleading does not prejudice the defendants. Plaintiff shall immediately file the second amended complaint. After the second amended complaint is filed, the Court will take the motion to quash set for March 28, 2013 and Demurrer set for April 2, 2013 off calendar as moot. Defendants may file new responses to the second amended complaint after it is served. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF BITE COMMUNICATIONS LLc's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY WANG VS. BANK OF AMERICA, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bank Of America, Na'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2013, Line 20, Defendant Bank Of America, Na'S Demurrer To Complaint. The demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first, second and third cause of action on the ground that Plaintiff was properly charged for research is overruled. The Personal Schedule of Fees is ambiguous. Plaintiff has alleged a reasonably susceptible interpretation, which is sufficient to withstand demurrer. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239. Defendant has not shown that any of Plaintiff's claims are preempted. The demurrer to the third cause of action on the grounds that Defendant cannot convert Plaintiff's funds is sustained without leave to amend. The money deposited with the bank immediately became the property of Defendant and therefore cannot have been converted as a matter of law. When the fee was deducted is immaterial. See Arnold v. San Ramon Valley Bank (1921) 184 Cal. 632, 634. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action on the grounds that Plaintiff lacks standing to prosecute this claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead, if he can do so in good faith, actual injury resulting from the failure to provide the requested documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
